mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the past 6 months signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the past 3 months signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real GDP over the past 6 months and a 10% increase in housing starts over the past 3 months signal a strengthening demand for residential real estate and corporate profitability that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises > 5% in 6 months and HOUST rises > 10% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the past 6 months and a 5% increase in real GDP over the past 3 months signal a strengthening supply chain and corporate profitability that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 10% in 6 months and GDPC1 rises > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% and a 10% increase in housing starts over the past 3 months signal a tightening labor market and strengthening demand for residential real estate that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls < 4.5% in 3 months and HOUST rises > 10% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
